publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-12-12 18:25:54 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Correspondence, news clippings, and articles relating to George J. Hecht (Cornell University Class of 1917). Correspondents of note include John Lovejoy Elliot of the Ethical Culture Society and the Hudson Guild, William DeForest, James G. Blain, and Senators Hubert Humphrey, Lister Hill, Cleveland Bailey, and Robert Kennedy. Also included are three taped interviews and transcripts, 1974.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, news clippings, and articles relating to George J. Hecht (Cornell University Class of 1917). Correspondents of note include John Lovejoy Elliot of the Ethical Culture Society and the Hudson Guild, William DeForest, James G. Blain, and Senators Hubert Humphrey, Lister Hill, Cleveland Bailey, and Robert Kennedy. Also included are three taped interviews and transcripts, 1974.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Subdivisions: 1. Personal correspondence and family life; 2. Social welfare work; 3. Education and educational legislation; 4. Hecht publications.Arranged chronologically within subdivisions.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Subdivisions: 1. Personal correspondence and family life; 2. Social welfare work; 3. Education and educational legislation; 4. Hecht publications.

emph.ref: Series I. Personal Correspondence and Family Life
Series III. Education and Educational Legislation

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: George J. Hecht papers, #3473. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings, articles, and a photograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, clippings, photographs, and a Basserman draft

did.unittitle: Meyer Hecht and Gella Stern Hecht (parents)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, clippings, and a photograph

did.unittitle: Hecht children (Susan and George) childhood letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains 1927-06-06 and 1927-11-21 copies of Better Times Bulletins

did.unittitle: How The Greater New York Fund Happened to Get Started

Bipartisan Citizen's Committee for Federal Aid for Public Elementary and Secondary Schools
Emergency Committee For The National Defense Education Bill; Citizens Committee for Extension of the National Defense Education Act

Correspondence from Washington, D. C. to parents

The Children's Newspaper of London, England

did.unittitle: "Dean Russell's Inquiry Regarding Children, The Magazine For Parents, And the Replies He Received"
"A Suggested Plan For A Management Corporation To Conduct The Publishing Operations of 'Boy's Life,' 'The American Girl,' And 'The Parents' Magazine."

did.unittitle: "An Unpublished Biography: The First 70 Years (1895-1965) of the Life of George J. Hecht"
